The State Department plays a crucial role in shaping healthcare in our country. From setting medical services policy to providing curative health services and managing health policy and administration, they are committed to ensuring the well-being of our citizens.

The State Department is responsible for critical functions related to public health and professional standards. They develop policies on public health and sanitation, ensuring the well-being of our communities.

The Kenya Medical Training College (KMTC) is a State Corporation established through an Act of Parliament under the Ministry of Health, entrusted with the role of training various disciplines in the health sector to serve the Kenyan interests, East Africa and beyond.
